title: Achoriphragma runcinatum
taxonomy: Achoriphragma runcinatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a type of thistle native to the Mediterranean region, specifically in countries such as Greece and Turkey. The plant belongs to the tribe Cardueae within the aster family. Further classification of Achoriphragma runcinatum places it in the subtribe Carduinae. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered a member of the aster family.

intro: Achoriphragma runcinatum, also known as Acorus calamus's relative or as Acorus, is a type of plant that belongs to the Acoraceae family and is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The health benefits of Achoriphragma runcinatum include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular natural remedy. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Achoriphragma runcinatum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as essential oils, which are often used in traditional medicine and aromatherapy.
